page: Amazon Web Services c8gd.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. Built on the ARM64 architecture, it features 64 dedicated physical cores using the ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 model running at 2.8 GHz. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio. It includes a bundled 3800 GB local NVMe SSD for high-speed storage and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps under the Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in memory bandwidth, with both cached and uncached read/write operations ranking in the top 10%. Multi-core processing is highly efficient, as demonstrated by top-tier results in Geekbench multi-core tests, software compilation with Clang, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Database and key-value store operations are exceptionally strong, with Redis SET operations and Passmark database benchmarks achieving top-tier status. Single-core performance and cryptographic operations remain in the average tier.

The ARM64 architecture and dedicated core allocation offer high compute density and energy efficiency, translating to favorable qualitative cost efficiency for scale-out workloads. The combination of high-speed local NVMe storage and top-tier memory bandwidth makes this instance well-suited for high-throughput databases, software compilation pipelines, static web serving, and medium-scale LLM inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 64 dedicated ARM64 physical cores powered by AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processors running at 2.8 GHz. It is configured with 128.0 GB of memory, a 3800 GB local NVMe SSD, and a 30 Gbps baseline network speed managed by the Nitro hypervisor. Benchmark results show top-tier performance in memory bandwidth, multi-core software compilation, and database operations, including Redis SET throughput. While single-core performance is average, the instance excels in multi-threaded tasks and high-throughput data processing. This hardware profile offers strong qualitative cost efficiency for resource-intensive workloads. It is highly suitable for database hosting, CI/CD compilation pipelines, static web serving, and medium-scale language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.16xlarge is a compute-optimized ARM64 instance featuring 64 Graviton physical cores, 128 GB RAM, 3800 GB local NVMe SSD storage, and top-tier memory and database performance.

tagline: A high-density ARM64 compute instance featuring dedicated physical cores, high-speed local NVMe storage, and top-tier memory bandwidth for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated ARM64 physical cores utilizing AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processors.
128.0 GB of system memory paired with 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
Top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-core software compilation performance.
Excellent throughput for database operations and Redis key-value stores.
30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor.
Optimized for high-throughput web serving, CI/CD pipelines, and database workloads.
